identity.name: Prisma Browser — Cross-Origin SaaS Copy-Paste DLP (T1567)
identity.summary: A managed user copies a record from a sanctioned CRM origin and pastes it into a personal webmail origin. Prisma Browser tags the clipboard write with the sanctioned-CRM origin, detects the destination-origin mismatch at paste time, and XSIAM stitches the copy and paste events by clipboard payload hash into one cross-origin DLP incident — browser-native insider exfiltration detection.
identity.description: Browser copy-paste is the most common insider exfiltration channel and is invisible to network DLP because the data never leaves as a file. Prisma Browser instruments the clipboard inside the rendering context: a copy from the sanctioned CRM (`crm.cortexsim-test.invalid/account/AC-12345`, selector `#account-detail`) tags the clipboard write with the source origin and a payload hash; the subsequent paste into personal webmail (`mail.cortexsim-test.invalid/compose`, `textarea[name="body"]`) is recognized as a cross-origin movement because the destination origin differs from the clipboard-source tag. The cortex-browser-attacker EAL plugin drives navigate → copy → navigate → paste, emitting `prisma_browser_events` with `action` of copy/paste, `origin`, `clipboard_origin`, `clipboard_payload_hash`, and `data_classification`. Detection layers: a BIOC on cross-origin paste from sanctioned CRM into webmail; analytics on clipboard-origin vs. paste-destination mismatch; and two XSIAM correlation analytics that stitch the copy and paste by clipboard payload hash and render both origins on one incident timeline. A correlation rule fuses the BIOC with the origin-mismatch analytic into one exfiltration incident.

execution.framework_reference_id: browser_attack_runner — campaign scenarios/browser/campaigns/saas-cross-origin-dlp.yml (navigate + copy + navigate + paste actions)

prerequisites.description: Managed Prisma Browser with clipboard DLP enabled; reporting telemetry to XSIAM.
prerequisites.check_command: python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe browser_attack_runner | jq -e '.eal_targets | length > 0'

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-BROWSER-004 (cortex-browser-attacker installed; CRM + webmail origins reachable).

payload.code:
# Drives the managed Prisma Browser through the cross-origin DLP campaign.
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/sim-browser-004-eal.yml --live
# Campaign: navigate crm.../account/AC-12345 ; copy #account-detail
#           navigate mail.../compose ; paste into textarea[name="body"]

expected_artifacts.description: Clipboard write tagged with the sanctioned-CRM source origin.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: action = "copy" and origin = "crm.cortexsim-test.invalid" and clipboard_payload_hash != null

expected_artifacts.description: Paste into personal webmail whose origin differs from the clipboard-source tag.

biocs.name: Prisma Browser — cross-origin paste from sanctioned CRM into personal webmail (DLP rule)
biocs.description: Prisma Browser — cross-origin paste from sanctioned CRM into personal webmail (DLP rule). A paste whose clipboard-source origin is a sanctioned CRM lands in a personal webmail destination origin.
biocs.logic:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter action = "paste"
| filter clipboard_origin in ("crm.cortexsim-test.invalid", "crm.corp.example.com", "salesforce.com")
| filter origin in ("mail.cortexsim-test.invalid", "mail.google.com", "outlook.live.com")
| filter clipboard_origin != origin

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight: plugin metadata visible to operator
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter campaign_id = "BC-BROWSER-004"
| filter action in ("navigate", "copy", "paste")
| fields _time, campaign_id, action, origin, clipboard_origin, url
| sort asc _time

xql_queries.name: Prisma Browser — clipboard origin tag mismatch with paste destination origin
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action = "paste"
| filter clipboard_origin != null and clipboard_origin != origin
| fields _time, user, clipboard_origin, origin, url, clipboard_payload_hash, data_classification
| sort desc _time

xql_queries.name: XSIAM stitching — copy + paste correlated by clipboard payload hash
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action in ("copy", "paste")
| filter clipboard_payload_hash != null
| comp count_distinct(action) as action_count, values(origin) as origins by user, clipboard_payload_hash
| filter action_count >= 2
| fields user, clipboard_payload_hash, action_count, origins

xql_queries.name: XSIAM — stitched cross-origin DLP incident with both origins on the timeline
xql_queries.query:
dataset = prisma_browser_events
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter action in ("copy", "paste")
| filter clipboard_payload_hash != null
| comp min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen, values(origin) as origins, values(action) as actions by user, clipboard_payload_hash
| filter array_length(origins) >= 2
| fields user, clipboard_payload_hash, first_seen, last_seen, origins, actions

correlation_rules.name: Browser Cross-Origin Clipboard DLP — CRM to Webmail
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(Prisma Browser — cross-origin paste from sanctioned CRM into personal webmail (DLP rule))

analytics_modules: Prisma Browser — Clipboard origin tagging + DLP
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Clipboard payload-hash copy/paste stitching

products.evidence_query: dataset = prisma_browser_events | filter action = "paste" and clipboard_origin != origin | fields _time, user, clipboard_origin, origin
Correlation CR-BROWSER-0004 stitches copy+paste by clipboard payload hash into one cross-origin DLP incident.

use_cases.name: Cross-SaaS Data Movement Detection
use_cases.description: Detect sanctioned-SaaS data copied and pasted into personal/external origins inside the managed Prisma Browser, stitched into one incident.

test_cases.objective: Cross-origin paste from sanctioned CRM into personal webmail fires the DLP BIOC.
preconditions: Managed Prisma Browser with clipboard DLP enabled.
Sanctioned-origin list configured (CRM sanctioned, personal webmail not).
steps: Run SIM-BROWSER-004 step-02 (copy from CRM, paste into webmail).
Observe the DLP alert and stitched incident in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `Prisma Browser — cross-origin paste from sanctioned CRM into personal webmail (DLP rule)` fires within 60s.
Origin-mismatch and both stitching XQL queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Copy and paste stitch by clipboard payload hash into one incident with both origins.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-BROWSER-0004` produces a single exfiltration incident.
Incident timeline shows both the CRM and webmail origins.

preventive_controls: Enable Prisma Browser clipboard DLP to block pastes whose clipboard-source origin is sanctioned and destination is not.
Maintain a sanctioned-origin list and treat personal webmail / file-share origins as untrusted paste destinations.
Apply conditional-access friction (warn/justify) on cross-origin pastes of classified data.
detection_engineering: Alert on any paste where clipboard_origin differs from the destination origin for classified clipboard content.
Stitch copy and paste events by clipboard payload hash to reconstruct the full movement.
Hunt for users repeatedly moving sanctioned-SaaS data into personal origins.

references.title: Unit42 - Insider Risk via Browser Exfil 2026
MITRE ATT&CK T1567 — Exfiltration Over Web Service
